theories.name: Comparison Local Theory
theories.core_question: What governs success and failure in comparison tasks under the current runtime regime?
mechanism_claims: Comparison tasks succeed when the system makes an explicit evidence-backed contrast instead of a generic preference.
predictive_claims: If concept support is absent, comparison tasks are more likely to fall back to weak preference-style outputs.
Repeated anomaly candidates suggest that local improvements may be insufficient for all sub-regimes of this family.
prescriptive_implications: Prefer contrast-preserving reasoning templates and evidence-backed comparison concepts.
Inspect contradiction patterns before adding stronger governance layers; some failure families may already be partially explained.

theories.name: Procedure Local Theory
theories.core_question: What governs success and failure in procedure tasks under the current runtime regime?
mechanism_claims: Procedure tasks often succeed through stable structure templates, while extra conceptual guidance may have low marginal value.
predictive_claims: Allowing many concepts in procedure tasks may increase activation without meaningful success gains.
Repeated anomaly candidates suggest that local improvements may be insufficient for all sub-regimes of this family.
prescriptive_implications: Default to structured procedural templates and keep concept activation sparse.
Inspect contradiction patterns before adding stronger governance layers; some failure families may already be partially explained.

theories.name: Synthesis Local Theory
theories.core_question: What governs success and failure in synthesis tasks under the current runtime regime?
mechanism_claims: Synthesis tasks fail when the answer collapses into a generic summary instead of preserving grounded support and the fact/inference boundary.
predictive_claims: Shortcut pressure and weak concept guidance will increase summary-style failures under tighter hidden contracts.

prescriptive_implications: Use synthesis-specific anti-shortcut concepts and keep observed/inferred structure explicit.

theories.name: Comparison Local Theory
theories.core_question: What governs success and failure in comparison tasks under the current runtime regime?
mechanism_claims: Comparison tasks succeed when the system makes an explicit evidence-backed contrast instead of a generic preference.
predictive_claims: If concept support is absent, comparison tasks are more likely to fall back to weak preference-style outputs.
Repeated anomaly candidates suggest that local improvements may be insufficient for all sub-regimes of this family.
prescriptive_implications: Prefer contrast-preserving reasoning templates and evidence-backed comparison concepts.
Inspect contradiction patterns before adding stronger governance layers; some failure families may already be partially explained.

theories.name: Procedure Local Theory
theories.core_question: What governs success and failure in procedure tasks under the current runtime regime?
mechanism_claims: Procedure tasks often succeed through stable structure templates, while extra conceptual guidance may have low marginal value.
predictive_claims: Allowing many concepts in procedure tasks may increase activation without meaningful success gains.
Repeated anomaly candidates suggest that local improvements may be insufficient for all sub-regimes of this family.
prescriptive_implications: Default to structured procedural templates and keep concept activation sparse.
Inspect contradiction patterns before adding stronger governance layers; some failure families may already be partially explained.

theories.name: Synthesis Local Theory
theories.core_question: What governs success and failure in synthesis tasks under the current runtime regime?
mechanism_claims: Synthesis tasks fail when the answer collapses into a generic summary instead of preserving grounded support and the fact/inference boundary.
predictive_claims: Shortcut pressure and weak concept guidance will increase summary-style failures under tighter hidden contracts.
Repeated anomaly candidates suggest that local improvements may be insufficient for all sub-regimes of this family.
prescriptive_implications: Use synthesis-specific anti-shortcut concepts and keep observed/inferred structure explicit.
Inspect contradiction patterns before adding stronger governance layers; some failure families may already be partially explained.
